entry.title: Re: How can I prevent materials with the deletion flag fr...
entry.content:
Hi Wendy, I ran this through our Eerly.ai SAP AI Consultant and got the following response.  Let me know if this answers your question.

SAP AI Consultant

In the SAP S/4HANA public cloud system, to ensure that a product with a deletion flag set does not show up in the material list, you need to follow these steps:

  1. Set the Deletion Flag in the Manage Product Master Data App:

    • Navigate to the "Manage Product Master Data" app.
    • Locate the product you wish to mark for deletion.
    • Set the deletion flag for the product. This action indicates that the product is no longer active and should not be used in any future transactions.

  2. Configure the Material List to Exclude Deleted Products:

    • The standard material list should automatically exclude products marked with a deletion flag. However, if it still appears, you may need to adjust the filters in your material list view.
    • Open the material list app or transaction.
    • Apply a filter to exclude products with the deletion flag. Typically, you can filter out items where the deletion indicator is set.

  3. Archiving and Deletion of Product Data:

    • For a more permanent solution, consider archiving the product data. Archiving moves the data to a separate storage area where it is no longer part of the active database but can still be retrieved if needed.
    • Use the archiving object relevant to material master data (e.g., MM_MATNR) to archive the product.
    • Navigate to the "SAP Information Lifecycle Management (ILM)" to configure and control the archiving and deletion process.

  4. Ensure Data Protection Compliance:

    • Make sure that your actions comply with data protection regulations. Use the SAP Information Lifecycle Management (ILM) to manage the blocking and deletion of personal data.
    • Refer to the product assistance for SAP S/4HANA on the SAP Help Portal for detailed guidance on data protection and archiving.

  5. Verification:

    • After setting the deletion flag and configuring the material list, verify that the product no longer appears in the material list.
    • Perform a search or run a report to ensure that the product is excluded from active listings.

By following these steps, you can effectively manage the visibility of products marked for deletion in your SAP S/4HANA public cloud system. For more detailed information, you can refer to the product assistance documentation on the SAP Help Portal at SAP S/4HANA Help Portal under Product Assistance Cross Components Data Protection.

entry.title: Re: Guide to use HANA NSE in the BW/4HANA system
entry.content:
hi @Dominik_Graus 

thanks for your comment and clarification, but you see, most probably SAP documentation is not clear/straightforward here.

also, a couple of points, when we are using the DTO warm option in the BW MT, it creates an additional indexserver, and moves the table here, even in the scale-in scenario, without additional nodes, I did traces of it, so if you interested you can write me on email and I can show it to you. So from my PoV, the functionality is not there, that's why we had to go this way.

I understand why SAP may not easily recommend the mentioned scenario, due to the fact the wrong use of it could create problems, but even if it will work perfectly, NSE advisor mostly recommends moving the indexes of the tables, which you cannot do with the standard functionality, and we gain the most benefits out of it.

so long story short, it would be cool if the vendors' documentation would be improved so that it would be clearer, and if the DTO would work "out of the box" together with NSE advisor, e.g. NSE advisor will also analyse a table group, so it would not be needed to check the single objects, but rather the whole group, like NSE.

so, if you are interested in the customer's feedback, I'm open for a call and discussion

kind regards,

Andrey

entry.title: Default sort on Promotion Management app
entry.content: Is there a way to configure the default sort of items in the Promotion Management app in CMC? Currently its being sorted by Creation Date and would like to default sort it to Name instead.

i am able to login in WNSCP same SFTP server while loginto data service designer i got above error message.

error message

could not initialize ssh transport layer for file location object<error -5> unable to exchange encryption key.

kindly provide proper solution..Thanks

entry.title: Re: Error in XML display in edocument Cockpit for SII Spain

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE backup server
entry.content:
No answers, just questions:

Do you see a change in the HugePages* values in /proc/meminfo before and after starting Backup Server?

Do you see a change in memory usage (by Backup Server) for dump/load when ASE is using huge pages?

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Component.getRouter() does not exist
entry.content: I guess something is missing in your project. Have a look at the TS sample here: https://sap-samples.github.io/ui5-typescript-walkthrough/step-30/README.html

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Dynamic Field Addition and Runtime R...

entry.title: Re: Making an OnPremise ABAP CI/CD with Github Actions
entry.content:
I was wondering what was that /ATRM/ namespace I found among the open source namespaces at SAP... now I know 😉

I've been working a lot recently on the CI/CD processes for an on-prem S4, but my focus is more set on the delivery of add-ons than on the propagation to target/production systems. I will definitely take a look at your TRM approach!

entry.title: Re: Guide to use HANA NSE in the BW/4HANA system
entry.content:
Hi @AndreyF,

Thanks for your feedback.

DTO warm tier can be either NSE or Extension Nodes. With NSE, no additional resp. dedicated HANA node is required.

In case of NSE as a warm tier, when temperature schema is on object level, for an ADSO with temperature 'warm', all relevant tables and related indexes are converted as page loadable objects. When temperature schema is on partition level, the same applies to all relevant partitions of that ADSO with temperature 'warm' and related indexes of these partitions.

Regards,
Dominik

entry.title: create Email template in C4C?
entry.content:
Dear All.

As I have no experience, kindly asking if any one could let me know what steps are needed to follow in order to create an email Template in C4C for Service Center?

Thank you in advance.

Regards,
Nikos

entry.title: Re: ASE has problems w/new disk controllers with 4 kbyte...
entry.content:
Note that, for servers having this problem, if you install ASE with a 4k page size, the problem will not occur. 

On my Linux server, the command lsblk -o "PHY-SEC,LOG-SEC" shows 4096 for both of these columns.  I think the LOG-SEC value equaling 4096 might be associated with the problem.

And just for review, the only way to migrate between page sizes is to use the sybmigrate utility (also see How to Use sybmigrate)

entry.content:
Hi Mark,

 Just this image that shows the memory  usage after a load operation.  

I can check for the other info

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

I am a graduate of BS Accountancy. I would like learn SAP and be certified for SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Financial Accounting.

I identify as a person in below categories:

• Women
• People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts
• People looking to switch their careers mid-life
• People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

I have started my learning journey and excited to finish it and learn more about the SAP.

Sincerely,
Krizia

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David,

My name is Tien and I'm from Vietnam. I saw your post just recently and deeply appreciated the opportunity you've given to the SAP community and those who want to make a new change in their career. I am a young woman and have been in the SAP industry for over 1 year as a SAP BI consultant and I'm eager to make a career change for my desired career path as a SAP FICO consultant. I want to use this opportunity to help me learn new knowledge for my career switch. 

Thank you so much and I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,

Tien

After updating each component to the latest version and try building the app.

Thanks,

Jamal

entry.title: Re: Report(s) that show After the Fact (ATF) Purchase Orders

entry.title: How PRod / Preview datacenter can have a feature down at the same time ?
entry.content:
We are experiencing severe feature down  in DC33 data center :

- in prod

- in preview

HR are unable to :

- terminate

- rehire

- update job/comp info

i would like to understand how it’s technically feasible that a change does impact both data centers at the same time .

is there some specific emergency changes that can be deployed in both datacenters at the same time ?

how can we find the latest change done from these category ?

If you need to create an email template in HTML format, I can recommend you the website brevo.com which can make it easier to create the template. On this website, you create templates and it generates the HTML code for you.

CM

do have any ideas?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How PRod / Preview datacenter can have a feature down at the same time ?
entry.content: It’s related to

entry.title: Re: How PRod / Preview datacenter can have a feature down at the same time ?
entry.content: It’s related to 3524107 - Unable to Perform (Hire/Job info/Comp Info/Termination ) Action - Page Loads Continuously Component: LOD-SF-EC-TER (OnDemand > SuccessFactors > Employee Central > Termination Details), Version: 3, Released On: 01.10.2024 SAP Knowledge Base Article Symptom Unable to terminate employees, Change Job info Action, Change comp info or Hire employee The system continues to load page indefinitely.

entry.title: Re: how to add bulk job code to the LMS?
entry.content: @lilymar, to import in mass, the Job Code Connector would be the option to be followed. If you're using the User Connector - SF, the job code value could be sent through it. If the value doesn't exist in Learning, it'll get created.

entry.title: Re: Single WF in SAP Data Services
entry.content: What are AL_engine and PID?

entry.title: Re: Create New Production Connector Error
entry.content:
Hi , 

Issue seems to be related to user authentication , Maintain same authentication type as configured in OPC server .

example : if authentication is allowed with username and password then configure the username and password in advance configuration within SAP DM configure production connectivity app and test. 

Hope this helps.

Thanks

Rahul

entry.title: Re: Why the Test Automation Tool (TAT) is a Better Choice...
entry.content: Didn‘t you answered question already by yourself in the sentence before?

entry.title: Re: For Story Learning reports, need to see item titles,...
entry.content: @james_aurich, to see the different locales the curricula, item, and program titles are populated: Go to the Learning admin side > Access the desired entity (ex.: Curricula) > Click the globe (Translate) icon > In this area, you could also manually add the text in the given locales. Reports like these ones could help with the verification of more entities: Curriculum Summary Data Audit Report (CSV) and Item Summary Data Audit Report (CSV). This scenario with Story and Learning is documented in the KBA 3267594.

entry.title: Monitoring in SAP Integration Suite(Cloud Integration)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have around 50+ custom iflow interfaces in SAP Integration Suite(Cloud Integration).

My functional team is asking to give KT on how and what all needs to be monitored on the Cloud Integration to ensure that interfaces are working fine and also check by themselves if some messages are processed successfully. Should we need to define custom headers so that they could search/filter themessages using custom header value in message monitoring. Could you please explain the below different error types in Cloud Integration monitoring?

 

 

They might ask each and everthing on monitoring and they are asking me to make presentation and show it to them.. can someone tell me if there are any such nice blogs regarding monitoring in Cloud Integration. I could make use that blog/article to explain them monitoring of messages..

 

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: Re: Flexible workflow PO update tolerance
entry.content:
On an S/4 HANA 2020 on-premise we created an implicit enhancement at the end of method IS_WFL_RESTART_REQD of CL_MM_PUR_PO_FLEX_WFL_HELPER.

This is performing manual tolerance checks on relevant fields.

At the same time, the customizing setting on those fields was deactivated, as the method would exit early in case of differences, as per the standard check.

 

The BaDi is still the recommended option, so do check if customer can update to S/4HANA 2021 or later to have access to the mentioned BaDi.

entry.title: Hello Experts, How is a logout implemented in SAP Build Apps?
entry.content:
How is a logout implemented in SAP Build Apps? Is it necessary to implement an HTML5 or CAP application to achieve this?

Thanks a lot

entry.title: Inquiry Regarding the Determination of Number Ranges for Contact Persons in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear Esteemed Experts,

I am currently encountering an issue with the assignment of number ranges for contact persons within SAP S/4HANA. At present, a grouping and an associated number range are maintained for contact persons.

When a new contact person is created, the contact person data along with the grouping can be observed in table BUT000.

Following a detailed analysis of the number range creation process for contact persons within the debugger, I observed that the number range for contact persons is generated in Include LSNR3F01 within the form local_buffer.

As illustrated, the most recent contact person has been assigned the number 9000100041. However, in transaction BUCF, the number status has been extrapolated to 9000100049.

Could you kindly clarify why the number status does not align with the last created contact person number?

I greatly appreciate your assistance and expertise on this matter.

Kind regards,
Emal

entry.title: Re: For Story Learning reports, need to see item titles,...
entry.content: Yes, we use the globes all the time for translations. What we need are the locale translations in reports. Currently, to see the different locales I would need to change my admin interface to the locale to see the translation in the report, which is generally not practical.

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, 3-system landscape
entry.content:
Hello, 
Thank you for the blog. 

In the 3SL and Activate methodology; we had some discussions about unit testing. In our projects, our customer is responsible of unit testing.

Should they proceed to unit test in the Customizing tenant or Test tenant?

After internal discussions, we highlighted that configuration is more flexible in Public Cloud, thus if we catch errors and issues early on, we can fix the configuration directly in Customizing and reduce management of transport and delta of configuration (likewise the Cloud methodology project in SAP Business ByDesign).

However, we want to keep Customizing more clean, thus not giving access to the customer to Customizing, and have unit testing in Test tenant (more like a traditionnal On prem methodology project i.e. SAP ECC). 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Was SAP UI Test Automation just killed by SAP API Hub and Solace?
entry.content:
You do automate test scripts which can be repeated multiple times with no or only minor changes. Usually this are regression tests which should assure that the solution is still working for the end user.

Unfortunately the normal end user will access the solution using some UI.

If you skip this layer your test script may pass but it may fail for the end user.

(No, I do not have a statistic.)

 

b) expand the number of people who can do SAP Testing - real SAP Testers: functional teams, developers and business users can also run SAP API tests (never the case with UI automated testing). 

Functional team will still use the UI layer. Business users will never do API testing and developers can easily get familiar with a test automation tool.

I cannot comment on Solace - cause I don't know this tool. Doesn't the customer has to pay for some licenses?

You can easily automate process steps in UI and API calls in one tool like OpenText UFT and compose a process or even end-to-end test.

entry.title: Re: For Story Learning reports, need to see item titles,...
entry.content: Right. Those audit reports would bring all translations of the entities, no matter the admin locale, but they're bigger in terms of data/lines.

entry.title: Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q4 2024
entry.content:
Hard to believe we are already entering Q4 2024 and with that we give you a glimpse of some of the new features in SAP Analytics Cloud. We have over 50 features for you this quarter but we will first take a sneak peek in to our Top 5 features in QRC4 2024. A full list of what is coming can be found on our Roadmap Explorer.

Bulk migration from classic stories to the optimized experience

The optimized story experience improves performance within SAP Analytics Cloud stories. We have enabled numerous features over the past couple of years and in this quarter we are excited to introduce support for the migration of multiple classic stories for SAP Analytics Cloud to the optimized experience. Some of the features include the ability to:

  • Overwrite the classic stories or save them as copies
  • Choose the location to save for converted stories
  • Get a log for the migration status

This new feature will help Customers save time and effort in migrating multiple stories and reduce migration downtime.

Grouping of analytics catalog assets

Another exciting new feature in our SAP Analytics Cloud catalog is the Quick filters in catalog to allow for faster and easier content discovery. End users can select a quick filter and search results get refreshed based on pre-defined filter sets. The quick filters are set by the admins for grouping content in a meaningful way and making the lives of end users easier. Feature is also available for catalog publishers and allows for consistent metadata tagging of content.

Plan Process Management - template support

An exciting new addition for our planning users is the introduction of a file repository template object for calendar processes. This enables planners to:

  • Save an existing process as a template (including subtasks and subprocesses)
  • Create a new process based on a template
  • Use templates in the event wizard to instantiate processes for multiple elements within the driving dimension
  • Support the transport of templates across tenants

 

 

Data Actions – dynamic planning area support

Staying with planning, in this release, we have taken further steps in our multi-phase refactoring of how user planning areas are created and managed. This is important as keeps the data size manageable for planning activities, which in turn improves performance and streamlines resource management.


This functionality has been building over the past few quarters in 2024, but specifically with the Q4 release, we are now supporting dynamic extension of existing planning area within a data action, while respecting boundaries defined when either DAC and/or data locking are enabled.


This new behavior applies to wherever the data action is triggered, and an existing planning area has been established, whether via stories, or the Excel Add-In

 

 

SAP Analytics Cloud System Overview Performance One Pager

Last but by no means least we bring you a Centralized Performance Dashboard. It provides a comprehensive, centralised solution for all relevant performance data, presented in a clear and organised visual format. This Performance One Pager consolidates key statistics from disparate tools into a unified view, eliminating the need for manual data gathering for administrators.

Learn more in this great blog by @thomas_fery 

 

There are plenty more exciting deliverables coming your way this quarter and we will be taking a deeper dive into these items in our What’s New video and blog series so stay tuned. For a full list of all features coming in QRC4 2024 for SAP Analytics Cloud check out the Roadmap Explorer

Want to experience new and existing features for yourself? Take the leap and start your journey towards making data-driven decisions with confidence by signing up for a free trial, today.

#sap #sapanalytics

entry.title: How to change the quote disapproval button tooltip text in C4C
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support team,

We would like to change the text of the tooltip for the standard notification's quote rejection button (in Portuguese). We were able to adjust the button within the TI screen using the language adapter, but we were unable to find a way to adjust inside the notification bell.

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to change the quote disapproval button tooltip te...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

The text of the tooltip buttons inside Notifications popup cannot be changed as the Adaptation Mode don't recognize any value in it.

If there is no Text Pool ID generated, then field cannot be adapted, which would be the case for any button inside Notification popup screen.

This is the expected system behavior.

 

Thanks,

SAP C4C Support Team

entry.title: Re: EAN Code & Category update in UoM, Material Master via LSMW
entry.content:
Hello, did you find an answer to this topic?

I have the same requirement, regards!

entry.title: ByDesign: Datasource for Production Confirmation including Scrap and reason
entry.content:
Hello all. Can anyone share details from data source where i can get scrap quantity and details from deviation reason and created on date/time?

The Yield and Scrap reports does not work for us because we want to know scraps reported by month until the production order remains opened.

i´d tried:

SCMPOHDR_ORDSTR_RPU01 - shows the scrap that generated stock. only show if we have good product reported.

SCMPOHDROPU02 - Production Orders/Lot: Dates and Quantities - does not show any scrap because work order is in process
SCMYASU01 - shows the total scrap even if the op is not closed. BY REPORTING POINT this is the yield & scrap report but it will not work because the date filter is OP end date.
SCMPTV01 - does not show scrap
SCMPOHDREXECU01 - Production Orders By Header Execution Details - does not show scrap

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Payment card master data made simple and secure
entry.content: Hi @Chennupati_Vidyadhari Did you found out how to configure this. I am also getting the same error.

entry.title: Re: Is Broadcasting Possible in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi, 

The best solution for broadcasting SAC Stories and AfO Documents is APOS Publisher Cloud. The APOS solution for SAP Analytics Cloud automates highly scalable processes for broadcasting personalized SAP Analytics Cloud content to end users in a collaborative team environment. Dynamic, security-driven processes with strict filters provide broadcasting capabilities for SAP Analytics Cloud and help administrators ensure recipients get only the data to which they are entitled. 

In the following video, you can learn more about the key steps in automating SAP Analytics Cloud report broadcasting:

https://www.apos.com/content/video-automated-sac-report-broadcasting-key-steps

Best regards, 

Esteban

entry.title: Re: Default sort on Promotion Management app
entry.content: Just click on title and it will be sorted.

entry.title: COD2405 I want to send an e-mail to account contact as a workflow.
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I'm using "Recipient Determination" as an account team.

But it also sends an alarm to the employee. The drop down box list that specifies "Recipient Determination" does not have a field to set account contact.
And there is nothing in "Custom Recipient determination".
And i hope the workflow sends an e-mail notification to the Sales employee only.

Can you tell me how to set it up?

 

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: COD2405 I want to send an e-mail to account contact a...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

It is currently not possible to add Sales Employee as Recipient Determination for Workflow Rules as per C4C standard design.

As a workaround, you shall try using 'Owner' field or use BADI -"ExitForGettingWorkflowRecipient".

 

I hope this helps.

 

Thanks,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: For Story Learning reports, need to see item titles,...
entry.content: I learned something new about the audit reports. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: print push integration (SAP_COM_0467)
entry.content:
Hi JP,

To be honest i thought they had discouraged this communication arrangement in favor of 466 (pull).

But it is good to know it is still available.

Can you check the help? What do you have set for your notification? https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b0f53dacc895493ebd634666fab2cd96.html?locale=en-US

 

  1. Enter a name and a description for the queue. Choose a format and make sure to select the name you chose for your communication arrangement in step 4. Set the notification type: When print queue items are added to the queue, a notification call is sent out. Here you can decide whether you want notifications for a certain number of items or for every single new item. Leave the other settings as they are and save your test queue by clicking Create.

 

I would recommend to create a ticket if you don't see the notification as you expect.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Festkomma-Arithmetik in ABAP unter SAP S/4HANA: Was Sie wissen sollten
entry.content:
Fixed-point arithmetic is an important feature in ABAP programming, especially when it comes to calculating and processing decimal numbers. Under SAP S/4HANA, the significance of fixed-point arithmetic remains unchanged, continuing to be a key concept to ensure the accuracy of calculations in ABAP programs. In this blog post, we’ll discuss what fixed-point arithmetic is, how it works in SAP S/4HANA, and what specific considerations to keep in mind.

1. What is Fixed-Point Arithmetic in ABAP?

Fixed-point arithmetic is a calculation mode that ensures decimal numbers are processed with a fixed number of decimal places. In ABAP, this is especially important to avoid precision errors that could occur when performing numeric operations with floating-point numbers.

Fixed-point arithmetic can be enabled or disabled in an ABAP program. When enabled, numerical calculations maintain the defined number of decimal places, resulting in more accurate outcomes.

2. Enabling Fixed-Point Arithmetic

In SAP S/4HANA, fixed-point arithmetic can be enabled in the program properties in transaction SE38 or SE80 for each ABAP program individually. This is done by setting the "Use Fixed-Point Arithmetic" option in the program properties.

The USING FIXED-POINT ARITHMETIC switch ensures that ABAP applies fixed-point arithmetic when working with numeric fields such as P (Packed Number) or DEC (Decimal).

3. How Does Fixed-Point Arithmetic Work in S/4HANA?

Under SAP S/4HANA, fixed-point arithmetic behaves similarly to previous SAP versions. However, there are some optimizations and performance improvements, particularly in relation to the HANA database. Fixed-point arithmetic primarily affects calculations carried out on internal tables, local variables, or database operations.

  • Accuracy in Calculations: When fixed-point arithmetic is enabled, the number of decimal places is retained, ensuring rounding errors are avoided.
  • Divisions and Multiplications: In these operations, the number of decimal places is automatically adjusted to ensure precise calculations.
  • Overflow Control: Fixed-point arithmetic helps prevent overflows that can occur when working with floating-point numbers or large numeric values.

4. Practical Benefits of Fixed-Point Arithmetic

Accuracy in Financial Calculations

Fixed-point arithmetic is often used in finance and business-related programs, where precision is crucial. In accounting or dealing with monetary amounts, calculations must be accurate to the cent, which is why fixed-point arithmetic is commonly enabled in many ABAP applications.

Avoiding Rounding Errors

A common issue when using floating-point numbers is the occurrence of rounding errors. With fixed-point arithmetic, the number of decimal places is fixed, minimizing the risk of rounding errors.

Consistency in Database Processing

In SAP S/4HANA, which uses the HANA database, fixed-point arithmetic ensures that calculations remain consistent between the application layer (ABAP) and the database layer (HANA). This is especially important when processing large amounts of data in real-time.

5. Specific Considerations in SAP S/4HANA

While the functionality of fixed-point arithmetic in SAP S/4HANA largely remains unchanged, there are a few things to keep in mind:

  • Performance Optimizations: Since SAP S/4HANA is based on the HANA database, calculations are significantly faster, even with fixed-point arithmetic enabled. However, care should still be taken to avoid unnecessary system load in computation-intensive operations.
  • Focus on Data Types: When working with large datasets or defining numeric fields, the choice of the correct data type (e.g., P for Packed Number) should be made carefully to leverage the benefits of fixed-point arithmetic.

6. When Should Fixed-Point Arithmetic Not Be Used?

There are scenarios where fixed-point arithmetic may not be required or could even be counterproductive:

  • Scientific Calculations: In cases where very high precision for large or very small numbers is required, the use of floating-point numbers is more appropriate.
  • Performance-Critical Applications: In programs focused on maximum performance, where small rounding errors are tolerable, fixed-point arithmetic may be unnecessary.

Conclusion:

Fixed-point arithmetic in ABAP remains a key tool for precise decimal calculations, even under SAP S/4HANA. It is indispensable in business-critical areas such as accounting or controlling to avoid rounding errors and inaccuracies. Thanks to the optimizations in S/4HANA and the performance of the HANA database, users benefit from high performance without sacrificing precision.

Understanding and correctly applying fixed-point arithmetic is essential for any ABAP developer working with financial or precise numeric data.

 

Question for the Community: What has been your experience with fixed-point arithmetic in SAP S/4HANA? Do you frequently use it in your projects, or have you encountered situations where it became problematic? Let us know your thoughts and best practices!

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Bonded Warehouse Outbound Process (Differentiation of stock types)
entry.content: hi Hari , unless i am missing some major functionality, the BWH inventory management is done in GTS. when a material document is saved in feeder (S/4, ECC) it get transferred to GTS if the movement type is activated in the feeder GTS plug in configuration; there are two important tables in feeder /SAPSLL/ECPPA and /SAPSLL/ECPIR3 which contain respectively the materials relevant for BWH transferred from GTS and the initial stock indicator. if the plant/sloc + the materials in these tables are set up correctly the transferred document in GTS is managed under the BWH (Customs ID) and the inventory movement is recorded as DUP or DP stock. vast subject; i hope i answer in the right direction

entry.title: Re: Microsoft Office Integration + Scheduling Analysis for Office Reports in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: Thank you very much for sharing this excellent summary and for the update on the Microsoft Office integration with SAP Analytics Cloud. It's great to see that SAP continues to innovate with the new add-in for Excel, especially with advancements in connectivity to SAP BW and SAP HANA, as well as improvements in conditional formatting and support for asymmetric reporting.

entry.title: Re: New Financial Accounting for Service Management in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
there is now a new blog available about the Advanced Execution scenario available in OP: blog about Financial Accounting 4 advanced execution scenario 

@klausloehnert1 : please check, if this will help you

@janehuang : for item based accountig only EBRR works as rev rec method. There is currently no settlement available, thus only margin analyis works for market segment reporting by workig with attributed market segment for the service order assigned postings.

entry.title: Re: fiscal representative concept-Intrastat
entry.content: hi Venu,vast topic, but the guiding rule is the plant address in the sales order and of course the company code which shld have a VAT in the plant country. if the plant used has address different than the physical location of the goods (country of disaptch) then we cant expect SAP to know which is the correct country. Ideally, the plant (even a dummy) shld represent the country of dispatch. If not, there is a way to force the INTRA_REL flag in the invoice table VBRK. once INTRA_REL is set, the Intrastat report will select the billing item and try to create Intrastat declaration for the VAT of the country in which this plant is located. this VAT can be a plant abroad - for example compnay code can or shld have VAT in each country where they possess physical stock/location and therefore has obligation to report intrastat in this country (based on their VAT). let me know if this helps

entry.title: Re: Condition exclusion groups
entry.content: i have created two logistic surcharges and created two exclusion groups so that : if one of the two is applied then the other one is cancelled.

entry.title: Re: Cannot publish product - no link to API Business Hub Enterprise
entry.content: Have you resolved this? Having the same issue.

entry.title: Re: Sales Order number not populated at the time of Adjus...
entry.content:
In SAP RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming Model), there are specific mechanisms for transaction control and numbering based on underlying frameworks, such as EML (Entity Manipulation Language). The situation you're describing seems to be related to the call sequence and numbering rules within the transaction processing framework. Here are a few things to consider:

  1. Transaction Processing Order: In RAP, various steps are executed within a transaction before the actual commit occurs. When you perform a custom action on an entity like I_SALESORDERTP, adjustments or changes to the data might only be visible after the transaction processing. The numbering of sales orders could happen in a later stage of the transaction (e.g., at a COMMIT), after your custom action has been executed.
  2. Late Number Assignment: In SAP S/4HANA, there is a concept called "late number assignment." This means the final numbering of documents (e.g., the sales order number) happens at the end of the transaction or just before data is saved. If your custom API adjustment takes place before this point, you may not have access to the final sales order number yet.
  3. EML and Numbering: When using EML to create sales orders, the numbering method might be executed separately from RAP transaction control. In this case, the numbering process for I_SALESORDERTP could occur after the custom action or API adjustment, as the final number is generated in a later step.
  4. Numbering Process in the API: Since you mentioned that your custom API triggers a numbering adjustment, you might be in an early stage of the numbering process. If the I_SALESORDERTP numbering method runs after your custom adjustment, it's likely because SAP ensures that all standard numbering steps happen after any custom modifications.

Suggested Solutions:

  • Delayed Access to the Sales Order Number: To ensure you have access to the sales order number, you could modify your custom API so that it either runs after the standard numbering method for I_SALESORDERTP or use a mechanism to trigger another action once the numbering process is complete.
  • Check Extension Points: Consider using extension points or specific hooks to adjust the timing of your custom API, ensuring that it runs after the numbering method.

If you want to dive deeper into the details of the transaction logic and numbering process, debugging sessions or a detailed analysis of the transaction method sequence could help pinpoint the exact cause.

entry.title: Re: B2B Integration Factory's Role and Value Adds in the B2B Cloud Integration journey
entry.content:
Hello @saurabhkumbhare, thanks a lot.
For now, please drop a mail. In that way we could also go into detailed discussions to better understand your specific requirements.
Looking forward to it.
Best regards
Mert

entry.title: Re: Report to find authorized Fiori apps for user's in SAP S/4HANA On-premise system
entry.content:
To obtain a list of all authorized Fiori applications for a specific user in the SAP environment, there are several approaches:

Transaction /UI2/FLIA (Fiori Launchpad App Analysis): This transaction provides a detailed list of the Fiori apps assigned to a user. You can also view the relevant authorizations and catalogs here.

Transaction PFCG (Roles): In the PFCG transaction, you can view the roles assigned to the user and check the authorization objects and Fiori catalogs within these roles. Fiori apps are assigned via catalogs in roles.

Tables:

AGR_1251: Contains authorization objects within the roles. You can filter this table by specific authorization objects to see which apps are available to a user.

AGR_USERS: Links users to roles. This table helps you find the roles of the user, which then define the Fiori applications.

Launchpad Content Manager (Transaction /UI2/FLPCM_CUST): This tool allows you to analyze the Fiori content and trace which applications and catalogs are available to the user.

The T-code /ui2/flc refers to the Fiori Launchpad Check Tool, which is used for general Launchpad diagnostics, but it is not suitable for displaying authorized applications for users.

I hope this helps. Please feel free to reach out if you have further questions.

entry.title: Re: Create Modify or delete role objects using BApi
entry.content:
To add or change objects in roles within an SAP system, there are specific steps and functions you can use beyond those you’ve already mentioned. Here’s a possible approach:

Use Transaction PFCG: In transaction PFCG, you can manually modify roles and add or remove objects. If you want to automate this process, various BAPIs and function modules come into play.

Function Modules for Changing Authorizations: PRGN_1252_AUTH_ADD: This function module adds authorization objects to a role. PRGN_1252_AUTH_CHANGE: This allows you to modify the authorizations of a role. This is helpful for changing existing authorizations or adding new ones.

Use BAPIs to Update the Role:

BAPI_ROLE_SAVE: This BAPI is used to save a role after editing, similar to using transaction PFCG. BAPI_ROLE_ACTGROUP_ASSIGN: This allows you to assign authorizations to a role. Checking and Reading Authorization Objects:

Use function modules like RFC_READ_TABLE to read information about authorization objects from tables like AGR_1251 (role authorizations) and AGR_DEFINE (role definition), just as you are already doing.

Mass Changes with /GRCPI/GRIA_MASS_UPD_ROLE: The function /GRCPI/GRIA_MASS_UPD_ROLE, which you mentioned, is also useful for making mass changes to roles, but for specifically changing an authorization object in a role, BAPIs are often the better choice.

Commit the Changes: After you’ve added or modified the objects, you need to ensure that you properly the changes. You can do this by using BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT after the role modification or saving.

If you cannot change a specific role or authorization object, the following might be relevant: Missing Authorizations: Check if you have the necessary permissions to modify roles. Object Anchored in Another Role: Sometimes, objects are set in higher-level or derived roles. This could explain why your changes aren’t taking effect.

If you have more details about a specific error or scenario, I can provide more targeted assistance.

entry.title: Is your business ready to tackle your data challenges?
entry.content:
Flexible approaches to data management such as a business data fabric still require well-managed data as a foundation. A semantic layer of active metadata and visual representations of data assets such as knowledge graphs are only as good as the quality of the underlying data.  Similarly, generative AI that is trained on corporate data is reliant on the underlying quality of that data, just as a house relies on sound foundations.  As such, corporations are exploring ways to maturing their data management practices.  However, every organization is different, and obviously there’s no one perfect way to invest in data management. Even so, it’s useful to have insights into the best practices being leveraged by your peers to calibrate what your current (and future) investments in these areas look like.

When it comes to broader data management initiatives, those business deploying best-in-class (BIC) data management practices see higher organization performance as compared to the average.

Best-in-class data management organizations vs. industry average organizations performance

What to see where you stand?  Here is a quick, six-question online Aberdeen assessment you can leverage to gain high-level insights into how your organization’s broader data management strategies compare to those of your peers.  Upon completion, you will receive a report tailored to your organization and how it compares to others in Aberdeen’s research.

Click here to get started! 

We hope the report is informative and provides you with some insight into the building blocks that make up a strong data management strategy.

Finally, here are some additional resources that can assist you on your data management journey:

  • Learn about what a business data fabric is, including how it can benefit your business:  https://www.sap.com/documents/2023/08/02af33dc-887e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html
  • Discover a step-by-step framework to implementing a business data framework: https://www.sap.com/documents/2023/10/4675c6a3-927e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html
  • Find out what's needed in a modern master data management (MDM) system:
    https://www.sap.com/documents/2024/08/8aff13da-d17e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html
  • Explore data management solutions on sap.com:  https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/analytics.html

entry.title: How can I update user password with sap bo rest api?
entry.content:
Hi

I want to update user password. I found below informations.

POST http://host:<port>/biprws/v1/users/password

Request body;

{
   "oldpassword":"",
   "newpassword":""
}

But I dont have a oldpassword I want to assign the new password directly without using the old one because I dont have oldpassword. How can I update user password with only using new password or how can I get oldpassword to using above method.

Thanks

entry.title: Dunning should stop after last dunning level
entry.content:
I would like to stop the dunning process once it has reached the last level. I have defined 3 dunning levels in a Public Cloud SAP system.

Let me explain this based on an example:

  1. Step 1: Let’s assume customer 4711 has three open items that have already reached the last dunning level (Level 3).

  2. Step 2: When I attempt to run a new dunning process, nothing is dunned, which is expected and correct.

  3. Step 3: Now, when a new open item is posted for customer 4711, I run the dunning process again.

  4. Step 4: My expectation is that only the new open item for customer 4711 should be included in the dunning process, and the previous open items (which are already at Level 3) should not be dunned again.

I have changed and tried many configuration. Can anyone of you please support how to make this possible?

Thanks!

entry.content:
In LTMOM I run the Simulation as follows:


My objective is to understand what data is causing the following error:

Just putting a watchpoint to message CQCL 008 is not a good option because it will stop only during the final log processing routine.

I'm usually debugging at the break-point before mapping but I haven't found the issue yet:

entry.title: Re: Creating a SuccessFactors Learning Story Report: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
Hi , I am not getting the option to select access rights . Could you please help me?

  • Select the type of access rights you want to provide to the shared report, such as Read, Edit or Full Access, and then click Ok.

entry.title: Shared component in the child catalog gets removed when the page in the parent catalog is synced
entry.content:
I have a scenario wherein

1.I have 2 websites (Site A and Site B) .

2. Catalog from site A is the master catalog and the catalog from site B is the sub catalog (child catalog) .

3.I have a paragraph component (P1) on one of the content page (CP1) in Site A . 

4. Create a new content page (CP2) in Site B and use the same component i.e P1 in CP2 and sync the page. The page shows up as expected in Site B with the contents of P1

5.Now go back to the page CP1 in Site A and make changes to P1 and sync the page . Content page CP1 in Site A is fine (with the updated content) but the component is removed from the content slot on CP2 in Site B.

Is there a way to retain the component on CP2 when P1 that is present in CP1 is modified and synced ?  

Any pointers and insights would be appreciated. Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Issue with Deep Entity Request in CAP (cds.validate issue - cds v 8.3.0)
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’ve established a basic parent-child association in my SAP on-premise backend and am consuming this OData service in my CAP application. However, while attempting to send a deep entity request to the backend, I consistently encountered the following error message:

'Property "0" does not exist in XXXItemSet'.

At first, I suspected an issue with our backend design, but after reviewing it thoroughly, we turned our attention to CAP since nothing seemed amiss with the backend.

The error message originated from the file /node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/linked/validate.js, and after some debugging, we identified the problematic check at line 202 (@sap/cds version 8.3.0):

 

 

else if (d['@cds.validate'] !== false) d.validate(data[each], path_, ctx)

 

 

At this point, the system loops through the metadata elements and their properties (those from the CSN file) for the parent entity set (XXXSet), which works fine. However, when it reaches the association and attempts to validate its elements, it fails. The system assumes that there is a @CDS.validate property in the association, but when it can't find it, it misinterprets the element list, and misunderstands the first element in the child item set as the property "0" (basically the first array count), causing the error.

To test this, I modified the CSN file by manually appending the @CDS.validateas false property to the generated association element. This allowed me to bypass the check and proceed with the process, eventually reaching the backend method successfully. Here’s the updated part of the CSN file:

 

    "XXXItemSet": {
     "@cds.validate": false,
     "type": "cds.Association",
     "target": "Dummy_SRV.XXXItemSet",
     "cardinality": {
      "max": "*"
     },
     "keys": []
    },

 

 

According to the Capire documentation the @CDS.validate functionality was introduced in the June 2024 (cds 8.0.3) release. If we downgrade to cds version 7.9.4, the issue does not occur, which leads me to believe that this might either be a bug related to deep entity creation or there’s an additional step we’ve missed with this new validation process.
 
We would greatly appreciate any explanation or solution that avoids downgrading to the previous release or manually editing the CSN file.

Any guidance or insights would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks!
Merve

entry.title: Re: Default sort on Promotion Management app
entry.content: I know you can click on the column name for it to be sorted that way. I was looking for a way if there is any, so that the default sort would be for a column I specify. This is for the promotion management app in CMC

entry.title: Re: Fixed-Point Arithmetic in ABAP under SAP S/4HANA: What You Should Know
entry.content:
LOL GenAI hallucinations 😄

If you don't know ABAP, please don't post content that you didn't check.

Also, please read the SAP Community Rules of Engagement: "If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag GenAI Assisted Content. For content where it's not possible to add a user tag (e.g., answers), you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content."

entry.title: Re: How can I update user password with sap bo rest api?
entry.content: Not possible for security reasons, if you do not have the old password, you cannot change the password

entry.title: Re: Issue with Deep Entity Request in CAP (cds.validate issue - cds v 8.3.0)
entry.content: Note: The cds_validate property was also set to false in the "cds/features" configuration within the package.json, as outlined in the attached June 2024 Capire documentation. However, did not have a positive effect on the issue.

entry.title: Re: Memory and CPU Consumption in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: Have you checked this, if there is an answer to what you are looking for: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/managing-performance-in-sap-analytics-cloud?

entry.title: Re: How can I update user password with sap bo rest api?
entry.content: IUser user = (IUser)tmpUser; user.setNewPassword(MD5.getUserPassword(userID)); This code, written using Java SDK, does not require the old password. Isn't there an equivalent for this in the rest service?

entry.title: Re: How PRod / Preview datacenter can have a feature down...
entry.content: I agree Philippe, I don't have an answer, but I do have the problem so glad we're not alone! We are DC57 and experiencing the same. I am really hoping that such a significant outage will mean that it is resolved by the morning - even if they won't call it outage, but being unable to execute even the most basic HR transactions in an HR system = outage in my view.

entry.title: Re: Enabling your workforce for the future with the new integrated learning experience
entry.content:
Hi @fabian_ , 

Great questions! Let me try to share some more information about how this works.  

For example, if I search for "management," will the search results display all relevant courses, regardless of the language they are in? Specifically, if a course is only available in French, will the search display the French version, or is there a filter for language-specific results?

The new search will look for matches across all languages and will provide a boost for the users language. Results will show based on the relevance of the keyword entered by the user compared to learning activities in their library. If "management" is the keyword entered by the user and there is a French learning titled "management" they can find it. 

Additionally, if the course is available in multiple languages, will the system prioritize displaying the course in the language I have set as my default?

If the title has been defined in multiple languages then, 

  • The results will show the title of the learning activity that has the best match. 
  • In case there is no best match, the system will show the title in the users locale. 
  • In case the users locale is not available for the learning, the entity ID will be displayed instead of a title.  

If the title has been defined in only one language then, 

  • The title will show in that language as it is the only one available.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Focus: SAP BTP Kyma Runtime
entry.content:
So, what Kyma logo is what: with six corners like here and with seven corners as at https://github.com/kyma-project/kyma? 

7️⃣ ↔️ 6️⃣

entry.title: Re: How can I update user password with sap bo rest api?
entry.content: You may want to look into the "Modifying User Details" section of the RESTful Web Service Developer Guide. The one you listed only modifies it for the current user which would be the one you used when you got the token. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESSOBJECTS_BUSINESS_INTELLIGENCE_PLATFORM/db6a17c0d1214fd6971de66ea0122378/ec5348346fdb101497906a7cb0e91070.html?version=4.2.5

entry.title: Re: Fixed-Point Arithmetic in ABAP under SAP S/4HANA: What You Should Know
entry.content: I am very familiar with ABAP and actively program in it. What makes you think that I don't know ABAP?

entry.title: Costs Associated with add'l Integration-Flow Service Instances?
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have an existing Productive CPI Installation and we're creating integration flows for a new external party to call on our Cloud Integration system. We'd prefer not to use API Management as our integration team is not familiar with it, we'd like to instead create an additional Service Instance that the external party can use as an OAuth Client. To do so I'm following the steps provided by SAP on help.sap.com.

When I bring up the dialog to create the additional new integration-flow plan it notes that "enabling a service might result in costs" (see below). I wouldn't expect having a second integration-flow plan would incur additional costs since it's SAP's recommended way of managing inbound calls from an additional party, but SAP is notoriously opaque about these thigns so I thought I'd ask the community... has anyone had an experience where adding an additional integration-flow plan for a new OAuth Client resulted in additional costs?

Thanks,
Alex

entry.title: Re: Responsibility Management: What Is It and Why Do You Need It? (1/5)
entry.content:
Greetings,

Very informative blog. I am trying to configure the functionalities of Workforce planning in responsibility management as the compatibility packs will be expiring. Is there a way where we can use our existing projects and WBS elements associated with it in Responsibility Management?

entry.title: Re: How can I prevent materials with the deletion flag fr...
entry.content:
Hi Rick, thank you very much for your detailed information.

For app "create sales order" (s/4hana public cloud), I didn't find the right way to adjust the filters when I searched materials via the search help/value help.

eg:1000012520 , has already was marked for deletion, but this product data still can be displayed via search help/value help.

And I found a SAP Knowledge Base Article as follows:

3111463 - In Manage Product Master Data, search for a product which is Marked for Deletion and gives "Invalid entry" (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition all versions; SAP S/4HANA Cloud all versions)

A product marked for deletion should not be available for further usage outside the Manage Product Master Data application. Thus these products are unavailable for selection via the search help/value help. But currently, it is not working as per design. In the user's s/4hana public cloud, the product data with deletion mark still was shown when using select material via the search help/value help. This is inconsistent with the knowledge “3111463 - In Manage Product Master Data, search on the product which is Marked for Deletion gives "Invalid entry". 

Do you think this is a bug in the user's system? How can I correct it? Thanks!

BR,

Wendy

entry.title: Re: Costs Associated with add'l Integration-Flow Service...
entry.content: You can create OAuth credentials tied to specific iflows. Just use the same instance, but create a separate OAuth account for the external party and assign it to the iflows they are allowed to access.

entry.title: Re: Monitoring in SAP Integration Suite(Cloud Integration)
entry.content: Just configure alerting in each iflow. They will get notified in case of failure.

entry.title: Ability to save files in a server folder from SAP DMS but be restricted to access thru OS
entry.content: User should be able to check out documents in DMS,  access those documents in file server folders, make changes and be able to save back to the folder from SAP.  But the users should not be able to access those documents directly using Windows explorer.

entry.title: Fixed Asset - Asset Under Construction
entry.content:
Hello, how do I transfer the values from an AUC asset (Asset Under Construction ) to a new Fixed Asset and close the AUC asset?  I have an Individual Material number, which is what I used to create the new Fixed asset, however, I am not sure how to move the costs and start depreciating on the new asset.  

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Fixed Asset - Asset Under Construction
entry.content:
Dear customer,

To achieve your goal you can make use of manual postings in fixed assets. In this case in particular, the manual posting "Complete Transfer of Individual Material".

For more information, please check the help document "Manual Postings in Fixed Assets ", section "Complete Transfer of Individual Material"

Link: Complete Transfer of Individual Material

Thank you.

My understanding is that the only way to control who can call the iFlow is via the User Role, and that user role is tied to the Service Instance. Is there another setting on the Integration Flow that I'm not seeing?

entry.title: Advice on Reducing DHCDC_RTSTORE Table Size in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Good day everyone,

We are currently consuming SAP business content in our Datasphere environment, with data being replicated from our on-premise S/4HANA system via replication flows.

Today, my SAP Basis team informed me that the DHCDC_RTSTORE table in S/4HANA has significantly increased in size. They have asked if there's a way to clean up or reduce the space used by this table.

Does anyone have experience or suggestions on how to handle this?

Thank you in advance for your help

 

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Setting up Oauth Token generation API for BTP API Management
entry.content:
Setting up Oauth Token generation API for BTP API Management.

 

  1. Login to BTP Integration Suiteà Go to ConfigureàAPI

 

 

  1. Click on Createà Fill in the details as given below:

 

 

  1. Click on create. Now go to resources and add a new resource as shown below:

 

 

 

  1. Now Click Save and then click on Policies.

 

 

  1. Now Click on Policy Template and Create and fill the details.

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Click on Create and Policy template is generated. Now in the Policy Editor Go to Proxy EndPointàPreFlow:

     

 

  1. Now Click on the “+” sign from under Security Policies for OAuth2.0

 

 

Fill the Policy Name and click on add. Policy has been added.

 

  1. Now click on the created Policy and update the details as given below.

 

Now click on Update. Policy for OAuth 2.0 token generation has been updated.

Now save the API and deploy.

Now add the created API to Product and Publish.

 

 

Now to API Business Hub Enterprise à My workspace à Create New Applicationà Add the Product

 

Use these API Key and Secret for connecting API and generating OAuth Token.

entry.title: Re: Costs Associated with add'l Integration-Flow Service...

I checked Note 3153804 (as I am on Cloud Foundry) and steps 4 through 6 of the resolution specifically instruct you to create a new Instance of the Process Integration Runtime to tie the User Role to, the image under Step 5 is the same screen as the screenshot in my original post. So this brings me back to my original question... if I create this additional instance, will we incur additional costs for it?

entry.title: Re: Bulk PDF Generation [thousands of unique customer sta...

entry.title: Re: Will 'Refund Control' = 'Create Credit Memo Request'...
entry.content: Hi Carlos,
